overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active and specialized social media presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, primarily focusing on academic research, computational methods, and disinformation studies. With a combined following of 3,580 and 7 recent posts, the profile demonstrates consistent activity, particularly around research dissemination and academic opportunities. LinkedIn boasts the largest follower count, while Bluesky emerges as the most engaging platform in terms of interaction rate per post.

contentThemes.description: Content focused on computational methods, algorithms, and studies related to coordinated link sharing, disinformation, and inauthentic behavior across social media platforms.
Announcements regarding new research grants, hiring for postdoctoral researchers, and opportunities for PhD students in computational social science.
Sharing newly published papers, upcoming conference presentations, and discussions on methodological approaches.

recommendations: Leverage LinkedIn's larger audience for major professional announcements (grants, hiring) given its high interaction volume for such content, and consider cross-posting these key updates to Bluesky and Mastodon to reach specialized audiences.
Continue to share research papers and tool updates (like CooRnet) on Mastodon and Bluesky, as these platforms demonstrate strong engagement for academic-specific content.
Actively engage with comments and replies on posts, especially on Bluesky and Mastodon, to foster community and encourage deeper discussions around research topics and opportunities.
Consider creating short video summaries or infographics for new research findings and job opportunities to potentially increase visual engagement across all platforms.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows the highest average engagement rate per post, suggesting a highly engaged audience receptive to new research papers, conference announcements, and academic opportunities. It serves as a dynamic platform for timely academic dissemination.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon provides a dedicated academic niche, showing good engagement for specific research tool releases and methodological discussions. While follower count is lower, the audience appears highly relevant and interested in technical research updates.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the largest follower base and generates significant raw interactions (likes, comments) for professional news such as grant awards and hiring calls. It is the most effective platform for broader professional announcements and talent acquisition.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
Our team has just released v2.0 of CooRnet, with improved algorithms for detecting coordinated link sharing behavior.
